title: Stabilize SurfaceCharts headless lifecycle tests under native validation
description: Native Validation failed on linux-wayland-xwayland-native after v2.38 lifecycle tests. The lifecycle tests intentionally block the shared Avalonia headless dispatcher, and solution-level test parallelism lets other SurfaceCharts integration tests contend with the same session.
acceptance_criteria: SurfaceCharts Avalonia integration tests run deterministically under native validation; lifecycle timeout tests no longer race other headless-session tests; no blanket skip or product runtime fallback is introduced.
close_reason: Fixed: SurfaceCharts Avalonia integration test assembly now disables internal parallelization around the shared headless session, lifecycle cleanup wait was widened, and focused plus full integration test project runs passed locally.

title: Align SurfaceCharts consumer-smoke support summary with Doctor parser
description: Milestone audit found packaged SurfaceCharts consumer smoke writes artifacts/consumer-smoke/surfacecharts-support-summary.txt using the older summary shape. Doctor parses v2.38 structured fields from that path, so consumer-smoke -> Doctor can report present evidence with null structured fields.
acceptance_criteria: Consumer smoke support summary includes GeneratedAtUtc, EvidenceKind, EvidenceOnly, ChartControl, EnvironmentRuntime, AssemblyIdentity, BackendDisplayEnvironment, and RenderingStatus prefixes; repository tests cover the producer/parser contract; audit can pass without changing chart rendering semantics.
notes: Scope: smoke/Videra.SurfaceCharts.ConsumerSmoke summary producer and focused tests/docs only. No renderer architecture changes.
close_reason: Fixed during v2.38 audit: SurfaceCharts consumer-smoke now emits the structured support-summary fields Doctor parses; Doctor now exposes assembly/display environment fields; focused tests and packaged build-only smoke pass.

title: Phase 338: Single Plot View docs and guardrails
description: Close v2.47 by aligning README, package docs, alpha feedback, generated roadmap, and repository guardrails around the single VideraChartView Plot.Add.* model and the deletion of old chart View components.
acceptance_criteria: Docs teach VideraChartView as the only chart View happy path; old chart View names are absent from public happy paths and type declarations; tests guard against compatibility wrappers/downshift paths/god-code drift; Beads export and public roadmap are regenerated.

title: Phase 337: Unified professional Plot presentation
description: Move professional axes, grid, legend, probe, style, precision, and evidence concepts onto the new Plot API for Surface, Waterfall, and Scatter.
acceptance_criteria: Plot-level axis/grid/legend/probe/style/precision APIs work consistently for existing chart families; evidence formatting names the unified Plot surface; focused tests cover presentation parity; no broad chart-family expansion or backend feature work is added.

title: Phase 336: Migrate demos and smokes to VideraChartView
description: Move SurfaceCharts demo, consumer smoke, and support capture paths to the single VideraChartView Plot.Add.* API. Remove old View-based happy paths from samples and docs.
acceptance_criteria: SurfaceCharts demo and consumer smoke instantiate VideraChartView only; Surface/Waterfall/Scatter demo scenarios use Plot.Add.*; support summaries still expose useful chart identity/evidence without referring to old View components; no compatibility sample remains.
notes: Completed by the Phase 335 merge on master. SurfaceCharts demo and consumer smoke now instantiate VideraChartView, surface/waterfall/scatter paths use Plot.Add.*, support summary reports VideraChartView and unified plot evidence, and repository docs/tests no longer advertise old View happy paths. Verification reused from Phase 335: focused Core demo/smoke/repository contract tests 54/54, SurfaceCharts Avalonia integration tests 110/110, SurfaceCharts Demo build 0 warnings/errors, rg old View names only in explicit absence assertions.
close_reason: Completed v2.47 demo and smoke migration to VideraChartView

title: Phase 335: Delete old SurfaceCharts View components
description: Remove SurfaceChartView, WaterfallChartView, and ScatterChartView as View components after the new VideraChartView foundation exists. Delete old View tests/docs/sample references instead of preserving wrappers or internalized Views.
acceptance_criteria: The old chart View component types no longer exist; repository references are migrated or deleted; tests guard against reintroducing the old View names in public happy paths; useful non-View math/render helpers may remain only when they do not preserve the old View abstraction.
notes:
Phase 335 paused before merge. Branch v2.47-phase335-delete-old-chart-views contains source-level deletion/rename: SurfaceChartView partials moved to VideraChartView, WaterfallChartView and ScatterChartView component files deleted, ScatterChartView integration tests deleted/migrated. Verified Videra.SurfaceCharts.Avalonia integration tests passed 110/110. Not merged because repo scan still shows old View names in demo/smoke/docs/public API contract/Core repository tests; those need coordinated Phase 336/338 migration before master can safely take the deletion.
Completed old View deletion on branch v2.47-phase335-delete-old-chart-views and merged to master. Removed SurfaceChartView/WaterfallChartView/ScatterChartView component entrypoints, migrated docs/samples/smoke/API contract/Core tests to VideraChartView + Plot.Add.*, and kept old type names only in explicit absence assertions. Verification: SurfaceCharts Avalonia integration tests 110/110, focused Core repository/demo contract tests 54/54, SurfaceCharts Demo build 0 warnings/errors, SurfaceCharts Avalonia build 0 warnings/errors, rg old View names only in absence tests.
close_reason: Completed v2.47 old SurfaceCharts View deletion

acceptance_criteria: VideraChartView exposes Plot.Add.Surface(...), Plot.Add.Waterfall(...), and Plot.Add.Scatter(...); the public happy path uses one View and one Plot model; internal render/layout helpers remain type-specific; no old chart View wrapper or compatibility adapter is introduced.
notes: Phase 334 complete. Added VideraChartView plus small Plot3D/Plot3DAddApi/Plot3DSeries foundation with Plot.Add.Surface/Waterfall/Scatter. Verified focused integration tests (2 passed) and Videra.SurfaceCharts.Avalonia build (0 warnings/errors). No old View wrapper, adapter, or hidden fallback/downshift path was introduced. Handoff: Phase 335 should delete SurfaceChartView/WaterfallChartView/ScatterChartView component types and migrate tests from old View names.
close_reason: Completed v2.47 VideraChartView Plot API foundation

Old chart View references span src View components, integration tests, demo/smoke XAML/code-behind, root/package docs, zh-CN docs, public API contract, Doctor/support-summary tests, and repository guardrails. Phase 334 should add VideraChartView + Plot3D + Plot3DAddApi foundation first; Phase 335 deletes SurfaceChartView/WaterfallChartView/ScatterChartView directly; Phase 336 migrates demo/smoke; Phase 337 unifies professional presentation; Phase 338 adds absence guardrails. Non-View helpers that may remain: runtime/controller/tile/cache/camera/projection/overlay/render helpers that do not preserve old View abstractions.

title: Phase 309: Scene authoring builder contracts
description: Add a small Core scene authoring API for concise static scene creation: typed buffer geometry, built-in primitives, material presets, object names/ids, and validation diagnostics. Keep it data-oriented and do not introduce an ECS or general engine layer.
notes: Phase 309 post-review completion: added missing plane/grid/polyline/point-cloud geometry helpers plus UV/topology validation coverage. Verification passed: dotnet test tests\Videra.Core.Tests\Videra.Core.Tests.csproj -c Debug --no-restore -m:1 --filter FullyQualifiedName~SceneAuthoringBuilderTests (5/5).
close_reason: Completed Phase 309 Core scene authoring builder contracts with focused Scene tests passing.

title: Phase 308: Visual authoring and chart presentation inventory
description: Inventory existing SceneDocument/InstanceBatch/material APIs and SurfaceCharts overlay/style/precision capabilities. Produce small API boundaries for Three.js-like static scene authoring and professional chart presentation without broad renderer expansion.
notes:
Phase 308 inventory complete.

Scene/Core boundary for Phase 309:
- Build on SceneDocument, SceneDocumentEntry, MeshPrimitive, MaterialInstance, InstanceBatchDescriptor, InstanceBatchEntry.
- Own src/Videra.Core/Scene/* and tests/Videra.Core.Tests/Scene/*.
- Keep implementation additive and Core-only where possible; avoid Avalonia runtime/demo churn.
- Do not introduce ECS, material graphs, animation, shadows, post-processing, compatibility layers, or hidden fallback.

Instance/perf boundary for Phase 310:
- Build on AddInstanceBatch, SceneRuntimeCoordinator, VideraBackendDiagnostics, diagnostics snapshot/formatter, PerformanceLab evidence.
- Own src/Videra.Avalonia/Runtime/Scene/*, src/Videra.Avalonia/Controls diagnostics files, samples/Videra.Demo performance proof files, and tests/Videra.Avalonia.Tests.
- Define draw-call intent and pickable counts precisely before expanding diagnostics.

SurfaceCharts boundary for Phase 311:
- Build on SurfaceChartOverlayOptions, SurfaceAxisOverlayPresenter, SurfaceLegendOverlayPresenter, SurfaceAxisTickGenerator, SurfaceColorMap, SurfaceColorMapPalette, and SurfaceAxisOverlayTests.
- Own SurfaceCharts chart-local presentation/precision files only; do not touch VideraView semantics.
- Main risk: duplicated hard-coded precision in axis/legend/probe formatting and brittle reflection-based overlay tests.

Proof/docs boundary for Phase 312:
- Own concise docs, demo proof surfaces, repository guardrails, and any remaining probe/readout precision closure after 309-311.
close_reason: Completed visual authoring and chart presentation inventory with parallel phase handoff boundaries

title: v2.42 High-Performance Visual Authoring and Professional Charts
description: Push Videra toward a high-performance, easy-to-use 3D visualization library and professional charting library. Scope: Three.js-like static scene authoring ergonomics, instance-aware performance truth, and chart-local professional presentation/precision. Avoid broad engine parity, animation, shadows, plugin systems, compatibility layers, or god-code.
close_reason: Completed v2.42 milestone: scene authoring builder, instance-aware authoring proof, SurfaceCharts professional style/precision, docs, and guardrails are complete.

title: Phase 305: Viewer fallback guardrails
description: Add or tighten focused repository/code tests that prevent default viewer software fallback from returning through Core, Avalonia options, minimal samples, and public docs. No compatibility layer or product feature work.
close_reason: Completed fallback guardrail docs/tests for explicit software opt-in and initialization-failure contract.

title: Phase 304: No-downshift contract inventory
description: Inventory viewer/backend fallback defaults, samples, docs, tests, Doctor/support evidence, and SurfaceCharts fallback vocabulary after v2.40/v2.41 changes. Classify intentional explicit fallback vs hidden downshift drift and hand off exact files for follow-up phases.
notes:
Phase 304 inventory complete.

Classification summary:
- Default no-downshift contract: Core/Avalonia defaults keep AllowSoftwareFallback false; docs already state explicit opt-in in README, Avalonia README, Core README, and extensibility docs.
- Explicit opt-in fallback: consumer smoke and fallback-specific tests intentionally set AllowSoftwareFallback true.
- Viewer stale/risky wording: ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/capability-matrix.md, docs/index.md, docs/package-matrix.md, docs/troubleshooting.md, src/Videra.Core/README.md, and repository doc tests need tightened language so software fallback is not described as default recovery.
- Support evidence alignment: docs/alpha-feedback.md, docs/videra-doctor.md, scripts/Invoke-VideraDoctor.ps1 if needed, and support tests should reserve FallbackReason for actual explicit fallback and use initialization/not-ready wording for default failures.
- SurfaceCharts boundary: SurfaceCharts chart-local fallback remains intentional, but README/demo/docs/tests should call it chart-local and explicit; cache load failures should not sound like hidden scenario fallback.

Parallel handoff:
- Phase 305 owns viewer/backend docs and guardrail tests only.
- Phase 306 owns support evidence and Doctor/readiness wording only.
- Phase 307 owns SurfaceCharts chart-local wording, demo cache-load wording, and SurfaceCharts documentation term tests only.
close_reason: Completed inventory and parallel handoff for Phases 305-307

title: v2.41 No-Downshift Contract Hardening
description: Epic for hardening the no-downshift contract after v2.40 fallback/compatibility reduction. Scope: lock viewer backend fallback defaults, docs/samples/support evidence, SurfaceCharts fallback vocabulary, and repository guardrails without adding product features or broad architecture changes.
close_reason: v2.41 phase work completed: inventory, viewer guardrails, support evidence alignment, and SurfaceCharts boundary lock integrated on master

title: Make software fallback explicit opt-in
description: Default backend resolution and host options should not silently fall back to software. Keep software fallback available only when callers explicitly opt in through AllowSoftwareFallback or equivalent options, and update docs/tests for the no-downshift policy.
close_reason: Completed: software fallback is disabled by default and only available through explicit opt-in.

title: Phase 292: SurfaceCharts Support Gap Inventory

acceptance_criteria: Document current support/reporting behavior, evidence vocabulary, lifecycle risks, and explicit deferrals before changing product code.
notes: Started Phase 292 inventory. Main owner: Codex. Scope: read-only inventory and planning artifacts; no product code changes in this phase.
close_reason: Phase 292 inventory complete. Findings recorded in .planning/phases/292-surfacecharts-support-gap-inventory; no product code changes. Handoff: 293 owns support report UX, 294 owns lifecycle guardrails, 295 waits for both.

title: v2.38 SurfaceCharts Support Experience Closure
description: Milestone epic: improve SurfaceCharts and Performance Lab supportability through focused demo/reporting UX, lifecycle reliability guardrails, and evidence alignment without broad renderer, chart-family, or architecture expansion.
acceptance_criteria: All v2.38 phase beads are closed or explicitly deferred; .beads/issues.jsonl and Docker Dolt state are clean; no broad architecture expansion is introduced.
close_reason: v2.38 phase chain complete: 292 inventory, 293 support report UX, 294 lifecycle guardrails, 295 Doctor evidence alignment, 296 cleanup.

title: Phase 290 diagnose test-tooling CI hang

acceptance_criteria: CI hang source is classified and either fixed narrowly on PR #84 branch or recorded with an explicit rejection/rollback recommendation; no broad blanket test skip.
close_reason: Phase 290 follow-up complete: PR #84 CI hang was fixed in eb77650 by replacing FluentAssertions set equivalency with SetEquals assertions and keeping xunit.runner.visualstudio at 2.8.2; PR #84 checks are clean.

title: Phase 290 fix SourceLink budget contract test

close_reason: Phase 290 follow-up complete: SourceLink package-size budget contract test updated in e2c807d; local PackageSizeBudgetRepositoryTests and release dry-run passed; PR #88 checks are clean.

title: Remediate test tooling FluentAssertions compatibility

acceptance_criteria: Videra.Core.Tests passes under PR #84 test tooling update; touched test projects are rerun or bounded follow-up is recorded.
close_reason: Phase 289 remediation complete: FluentAssertions assertion API fixed in commit 5013ff7; Videra.Core.Tests passed. SurfaceCharts Avalonia integration hang recorded as bounded follow-up.
